statements.notes: Synergistic vegetarian formula
Good health starts in the gut. Nordic Naturals Probiotic Gummies Kids provides a synergistic blend of probiotics, and prebiotic fiber, to help establish and maintain healthy digestive balance.

When a child’s digestive tract is properly functioning, it can better absorb and utilize the nutrients needed for growth, development, and strong immune function. Our unique probiotic formula allows good bacteria to flourish.
These are great-tasting gummies your kids will love!

Suggested Use: For 2–4 years, one gummy daily. For 4+ years, two gummies daily. Take right before a meal, preferably breakfast, or as directed by your health care professional or pharmacist. Please ensure child chews each gummy thoroughly.
Store in a cool, dry place, away from sunlight
Do not take if tamper-evident seal is broken or missing
Keep out of the reach of children

statements.notes: Synergistic vegetarian formula
Good health starts in the gut. Nordic Naturals Probiotic Gummies Kids provides a synergistic blend of probiotics, and prebiotic fiber, to help establish and maintain healthy digestive balance.

When a child’s digestive tract is properly functioning, it can better absorb and utilize the nutrients needed for growth, development, and strong immune function.* Our unique probiotic formula allows good bacteria to flourish.
These are great-tasting gummies your kids will love!

Suggested Use: For 2–4 years, one gummy daily. For 4+ years, two gummies daily. Take right before a meal, preferably breakfast, or as directed by your health care professional or pharmacist. Please ensure child chews each gummy thoroughly.
Store in a cool, dry place, away from sunlight

ingredients.name: Vitamin B6
ingredients.notes: Most common synthetic form, requires conversion to active P5P, which can be inefficient in some individuals.

ingredients.notes: Synthetic, inactive form. Up to 40% of people have reduced ability to convert it to active folate (MTHFR polymorphism).

ingredients.name: Vitamin A
ingredients.notes: Preformed vitamin A with 70-90% absorption. Synthetic but highly effective.

ingredients.name: Vitamin C
ingredients.notes: Standard acidic form, widely used

ingredients.name: Vitamin D
ingredients.notes: Preferred form, identical to the vitamin D produced by skin exposure to sunlight. Highly bioavailable and well retained.

ingredients.name: Vitamin E
ingredients.notes: Stable ester form, widely used in supplements and fortified foods due to its resistance to oxidation.

ingredients.name: Niacin
ingredients.notes: Non-flushing form, preferred for skin health and cellular repair without vasodilation.

ingredients.name: Vitamin B6
ingredients.notes: Most common synthetic form, requires conversion to active P5P, which can be inefficient in some individuals.

ingredients.name: Folate
ingredients.notes: Unspecified form; conservative scoring to avoid over-crediting.

ingredients.name: Folic Acid
ingredients.notes: Synthetic, inactive form. Up to 40% of people have reduced ability to convert it to active folate (MTHFR polymorphism).

ingredients.name: Vitamin B12
ingredients.notes: Synthetic form with cyanide group. Requires liver conversion. Most studied form.

ingredients.name: Biotin
ingredients.notes: Natural form, optimal bioavailability. Essential for metabolism of fats, carbs, and proteins.

ingredients.name: Pantothenic Acid
ingredients.notes: Stable synthetic form, widely used in supplements and fortified foods.

ingredients.name: Iodine
ingredients.notes: Most common and bioavailable form of iodine supplementation.

ingredients.name: Zinc
ingredients.notes: Good balance of absorption and cost. Well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Selenium
ingredients.notes: Organic form, best absorbed and retained by the body, as it's incorporated into proteins similar to dietary selenium.

ingredients.name: Chromium
ingredients.notes: Niacin-bound form; also supports insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism.

ingredients.name: Bacillus subtilis DE111
ingredients.notes: Clinically studied spore-forming probiotic with RCTs for immune modulation, cardiovascular health, and bowel regulation. Highly stable and resistant to harsh conditions.

ingredients.name: Vitamin A
ingredients.notes: Preformed vitamin A with 70-90% absorption. Synthetic but highly effective.

ingredients.name: Vitamin C
ingredients.notes: Standard acidic form, widely used

ingredients.name: Vitamin D
ingredients.notes: Preferred form, identical to the vitamin D produced by skin exposure to sunlight. Highly bioavailable and well retained.

ingredients.name: Vitamin E
ingredients.notes: Stable ester form, widely used in supplements and fortified foods due to its resistance to oxidation.

ingredients.name: Vitamin B6
ingredients.notes: Most common synthetic form, requires conversion to active P5P, which can be inefficient in some individuals.

ingredients.name: Folic Acid
ingredients.notes: Synthetic, inactive form. Up to 40% of people have reduced ability to convert it to active folate (MTHFR polymorphism).

ingredients.name: Vitamin B12
ingredients.notes: Synthetic form with cyanide group. Requires liver conversion. Most studied form.

ingredients.name: Biotin
ingredients.notes: Natural form, optimal bioavailability. Essential for metabolism of fats, carbs, and proteins.

ingredients.name: Pantothenic Acid
ingredients.notes: Stable synthetic form, widely used in supplements and fortified foods.

ingredients.name: Iodine
ingredients.notes: Most common and bioavailable form of iodine supplementation.

ingredients.name: Zinc
ingredients.notes: Good balance of absorption and cost. Well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Bacillus coagulans MTCC 5856
ingredients.notes: Clinically studied B. coagulans strain with RCTs for IBS and functional GI symptoms. Marketed as LactoSpore by Sabinsa. Spore-forming for excellent shelf stability.

ingredients.name: Bacillus coagulans MTCC 5856
ingredients.notes: Clinically studied B. coagulans strain with RCTs for IBS and functional GI symptoms. Marketed as LactoSpore by Sabinsa. Spore-forming for excellent shelf stability.

ingredients.name: Vitamin A
ingredients.notes: Preformed vitamin A with 70-90% absorption. Synthetic but highly effective.

ingredients.name: Vitamin C
ingredients.notes: Buffered form, commonly used in IV and oral supplements

ingredients.name: Vitamin D
ingredients.notes: Preferred form, identical to the vitamin D produced by skin exposure to sunlight. Highly bioavailable and well retained.

ingredients.name: Vitamin E
ingredients.notes: Unspecified form; conservative scoring to avoid over-crediting.

ingredients.name: Thiamin
ingredients.notes: Water-soluble synthetic form, widely used and well-established.

ingredients.name: Riboflavin
ingredients.notes: Standard synthetic form, requires conversion to active FMN/FAD in the body.

ingredients.name: Niacin
ingredients.notes: Non-flushing form, preferred for skin health and cellular repair without vasodilation.

ingredients.name: Folate
ingredients.notes: Unspecified form; conservative scoring to avoid over-crediting.

ingredients.name: Folic Acid
ingredients.notes: Synthetic, inactive form. Up to 40% of people have reduced ability to convert it to active folate (MTHFR polymorphism).

ingredients.name: Vitamin B12
ingredients.notes: Synthetic form with cyanide group. Requires liver conversion. Most studied form.

ingredients.name: Biotin
ingredients.notes: Natural form, optimal bioavailability. Essential for metabolism of fats, carbs, and proteins.

ingredients.name: Pantothenic Acid
ingredients.notes: Stable synthetic form, widely used in supplements and fortified foods.

ingredients.name: Calcium
ingredients.notes: Poor solubility and low absorption. Often used as a filler rather than a primary calcium source.

ingredients.name: Iron
ingredients.notes: Ferrochel/TRAACS chelated iron. 2-4x more bioavailable than sulfate. Gentle on stomach, less food interference.

ingredients.name: Phosphorus
ingredients.notes: Poor solubility and low absorption. Often used as a filler rather than a primary calcium source.

ingredients.name: Iodine
ingredients.notes: Most common and bioavailable form of iodine supplementation.

ingredients.name: Zinc
ingredients.notes: Good balance of absorption and cost. Well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Selenium
ingredients.notes: Inorganic form; less retained by the body and may carry a higher toxicity risk at high doses.

ingredients.name: Chromium
ingredients.notes: Inorganic form; poorly absorbed and generally less effective for supplementation.

ingredients.name: Inositol
ingredients.notes: Most abundant and biologically active form; supports cellular signaling, ovarian function, and mood.

ingredients.name: Boron
ingredients.notes: An organic form of Boron, bound to citric acid. It is well absorbed and generally gentle on the digestive system.

ingredients.name: Omega-3s
ingredients.notes: Unspecified form - assume standard fish oil.

ingredients.name: Vitamin A
ingredients.notes: Preformed vitamin A with 70-90% absorption. Synthetic but highly effective.

ingredients.name: Vitamin C
ingredients.notes: Standard acidic form, widely used

ingredients.name: Vitamin D
ingredients.notes: Preferred form, identical to the vitamin D produced by skin exposure to sunlight. Highly bioavailable and well retained.

ingredients.name: Vitamin E
ingredients.notes: Stable ester form, widely used in supplements and fortified foods due to its resistance to oxidation.

ingredients.name: Vitamin B6
ingredients.notes: Most common synthetic form, requires conversion to active P5P, which can be inefficient in some individuals.

ingredients.name: Folate
ingredients.notes: Unspecified form; conservative scoring to avoid over-crediting.

ingredients.name: Folic Acid
ingredients.notes: Synthetic, inactive form. Up to 40% of people have reduced ability to convert it to active folate (MTHFR polymorphism).

ingredients.name: Vitamin B12
ingredients.notes: Synthetic form with cyanide group. Requires liver conversion. Most studied form.

ingredients.name: Biotin
ingredients.notes: Natural form, optimal bioavailability. Essential for metabolism of fats, carbs, and proteins.

ingredients.name: Pantothenic Acid
ingredients.notes: Stable synthetic form, widely used in supplements and fortified foods.

ingredients.name: Choline
ingredients.notes: A basic salt form of Choline; it is cost-effective but has limited brain uptake compared to more advanced forms like Alpha-GPC or Citicoline.

ingredients.name: Iodine
ingredients.notes: Most common and bioavailable form of iodine supplementation.

ingredients.name: Zinc
ingredients.notes: Good balance of absorption and cost. Well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Bacillus coagulans MTCC-5856
ingredients.notes: Clinically studied B. coagulans strain with RCTs for IBS and functional GI symptoms. Marketed as LactoSpore by Sabinsa. Spore-forming for excellent shelf stability.

ingredients.notes: Stable ester form, widely used in supplements and fortified foods due to its resistance to oxidation.

ingredients.name: Vitamin B6
ingredients.notes: Most common synthetic form, requires conversion to active P5P, which can be inefficient in some individuals.

ingredients.name: Folate
ingredients.notes: Unspecified form; conservative scoring to avoid over-crediting.

ingredients.name: Folic Acid
ingredients.notes: Synthetic, inactive form. Up to 40% of people have reduced ability to convert it to active folate (MTHFR polymorphism).

ingredients.name: Vitamin B12
ingredients.notes: Synthetic form with cyanide group. Requires liver conversion. Most studied form.

ingredients.name: Biotin
ingredients.notes: Natural form, optimal bioavailability. Essential for metabolism of fats, carbs, and proteins.

ingredients.name: Pantothenic Acid
ingredients.notes: Stable synthetic form, widely used in supplements and fortified foods.

ingredients.name: Choline
ingredients.notes: A basic salt form of Choline; it is cost-effective but has limited brain uptake compared to more advanced forms like Alpha-GPC or Citicoline.

ingredients.name: Iodine
ingredients.notes: Most common and bioavailable form of iodine supplementation.

ingredients.name: Zinc
ingredients.notes: Good balance of absorption and cost. Well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Bacillus coagulans MTCC 5856
ingredients.notes: Clinically studied B. coagulans strain with RCTs for IBS and functional GI symptoms. Marketed as LactoSpore by Sabinsa. Spore-forming for excellent shelf stability.

ingredients.notes: Buffered form, commonly used in IV and oral supplements

ingredients.name: Vitamin D
ingredients.notes: Preferred form, identical to the vitamin D produced by skin exposure to sunlight. Highly bioavailable and well retained.

ingredients.name: Vitamin E
ingredients.notes: Natural RRR form, preferentially retained and utilized by the body, offering superior biological activity to synthetic forms.

ingredients.name: Thiamin
ingredients.notes: Water-soluble synthetic form, widely used and well-established.

ingredients.name: Riboflavin
ingredients.notes: Standard synthetic form, requires conversion to active FMN/FAD in the body.

ingredients.name: Niacin
ingredients.notes: Non-flushing form, preferred for skin health and cellular repair without vasodilation.

ingredients.name: Vitamin B6
ingredients.notes: Most common synthetic form, requires conversion to active P5P, which can be inefficient in some individuals.

ingredients.name: Folate
ingredients.notes: Unspecified form; conservative scoring to avoid over-crediting.

ingredients.name: Folic Acid
ingredients.notes: Synthetic, inactive form. Up to 40% of people have reduced ability to convert it to active folate (MTHFR polymorphism).

ingredients.name: Vitamin B12
ingredients.notes: Synthetic form with cyanide group. Requires liver conversion. Most studied form.

ingredients.name: Biotin
ingredients.notes: Natural form, optimal bioavailability. Essential for metabolism of fats, carbs, and proteins.

ingredients.name: Pantothenic Acid
ingredients.notes: Stable synthetic form, widely used in supplements and fortified foods.

ingredients.name: Calcium
ingredients.notes: Poor solubility and low absorption. Often used as a filler rather than a primary calcium source.

ingredients.name: Iron
ingredients.notes: Good absorption, 33% elemental iron. Common in supplements.

ingredients.name: Phosphorus
ingredients.notes: Poor solubility and low absorption. Often used as a filler rather than a primary calcium source.

ingredients.name: Iodine
ingredients.notes: Most common and bioavailable form of iodine supplementation.

ingredients.name: Zinc
ingredients.notes: Good balance of absorption and cost. Well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Selenium
ingredients.notes: Inorganic form; less retained by the body and may carry a higher toxicity risk at high doses.

ingredients.name: Chromium
ingredients.notes: Inorganic form; poorly absorbed and generally less effective for supplementation.

ingredients.name: Inositol
ingredients.notes: Most abundant and biologically active form; supports cellular signaling, ovarian function, and mood.

ingredients.name: Boron
ingredients.notes: An organic form of Boron, bound to citric acid. It is well absorbed and generally gentle on the digestive system.

ingredients.name: Omega-3s
ingredients.notes: Unspecified form - assume standard fish oil.

ingredients.notes: Preformed vitamin A with 70-90% absorption. Synthetic but highly effective.

ingredients.name: Vitamin C
ingredients.notes: Standard acidic form, widely used

ingredients.name: Vitamin D
ingredients.notes: Preferred form, identical to the vitamin D produced by skin exposure to sunlight. Highly bioavailable and well retained.

ingredients.name: Vitamin E
ingredients.notes: Stable ester form, widely used in supplements and fortified foods due to its resistance to oxidation.

ingredients.name: Niacin
ingredients.notes: Non-flushing form, preferred for skin health and cellular repair without vasodilation.

ingredients.name: Vitamin B6
ingredients.notes: Most common synthetic form, requires conversion to active P5P, which can be inefficient in some individuals.

ingredients.name: Folate
ingredients.notes: Unspecified form; conservative scoring to avoid over-crediting.

ingredients.name: Folic Acid
ingredients.notes: Synthetic, inactive form. Up to 40% of people have reduced ability to convert it to active folate (MTHFR polymorphism).

ingredients.name: Vitamin B12
ingredients.notes: Synthetic form with cyanide group. Requires liver conversion. Most studied form.

ingredients.name: Biotin
ingredients.notes: Natural form, optimal bioavailability. Essential for metabolism of fats, carbs, and proteins.

ingredients.name: Pantothenic Acid
ingredients.notes: Stable synthetic form, widely used in supplements and fortified foods.

ingredients.name: Iodine
ingredients.notes: Most common and bioavailable form of iodine supplementation.

ingredients.name: Zinc
ingredients.notes: Good balance of absorption and cost. Well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Selenium
ingredients.notes: Organic form, best absorbed and retained by the body, as it's incorporated into proteins similar to dietary selenium.

ingredients.name: Chromium
ingredients.notes: Niacin-bound form; also supports insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism.

ingredients.name: Probiotic
ingredients.notes: Generic form; adjust when specific form is known.

ingredients.name: Bacillus subtilis DE-111
